negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing at least one guardrail: regular state-cache TTL/idle environment configuration is absent. Other related guardrails exist, including zero-frequency scheduled call skip and scheduled incident trace bounded capacity, but the full compound invariant is not satisfied.
related_but_insufficient_code: due_index_bounds returns None when frequency == 0, preventing division by zero.
scheduled_incident_trace_config exposes enabled/capacity bounded by MAX_SCHEDULED_INCIDENT_TRACE_CAPACITY.

notes: Confirmed gap because compound invariant is not fully satisfied.
rationale: These candidates are independent guardrails around scheduler validation and operations; none is superseded by another packet candidate except the cache default mismatch already reflected in the statement.
statement: Regular state-cache TTL/idle settings are environment-configurable, commitment transactions are not rejected solely for TTL expiry in block verification, zero-frequency scheduled calls are skipped without division by zero, verifier obligation computation is deadline-bounded, transaction-pool calldata counters stay balanced across mutations, and scheduled incident tracing is enabled/configurable with bounded capacity.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set cache TTL/idle env vars and assert provider caches use them; current code lacks env handling.
Regression tests for zero-frequency due_index_bounds and incident trace capacity.
summary: Operational guardrails are partial; cache TTL/idle env configuration is missing.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Compound guardrail test suite; currently fails on state-cache TTL/idle config.
title: Operational validation guardrails remain current
